channel.title: Agent Nouveau — Facebook
channel.description: Agent Nouveau journal — Facebook editorial feed

item.title: ⟡ The Road That Is the Holiday
item.description:
⟡ The Road That Is the Holiday
Knysna and Plettenberg Bay sit barely thirty kilometres apart. Half an hour of driving, the two best-known towns on South Africa's Garden Route close enough to share a morning.
Which is the thing most people get wrong about the route. They treat it as a list of stops to collect before dark. We would make the case for the opposite. The road is the holiday. The forest, the lagoons, the mountains on one side and the ocean on the other... the drive is not how you reach the experience. It is the experience.
Our new journal piece on the Garden Route, and the case for travelling it slowly.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The best part has no name
item.description:
⟡ The best part has no name
Wilderness to Knysna is about forty-five kilometres, the road running past a chain of lakes and lagoons close enough to the verge to touch. Then Knysna to Plettenberg Bay, thirty more, the forest thickening and thinning, the ocean appearing and slipping behind a rise and appearing again.
Driven in a hurry, it is forgettable. Driven slowly, with the windows down and nowhere to be by a fixed hour, it is the reason to come. The towns at either end are lovely. The road between them is the point.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Give the road the days
item.description:
⟡ Give the road the days
The common mistake is the schedule. Three towns in a day, a photograph at each, a long drive after dark to make the next booking. The road punishes that by becoming invisible, just the dull bit between the parts that count.
Travelled the other way, with two or three nights in one place and the road itself treated as the day's plan, the whole thing changes. The mornings are slow. The drives are short. The light arrives in the late afternoon and asks nothing of you but that you stop and watch it. You come home slower than you left... and that is the version worth planning.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Valleys That Run on Afternoons
item.description:
⟡ The Valleys That Run on Afternoons
The first wine at the Cape was pressed in 1659. The vineyards that grew from it cover the valleys an hour east of Cape Town, and after three and a half centuries they have learned how to be unhurried.
We keep sending people to Stellenbosch and Franschhoek, and almost never for the scores. We send them for the afternoon. A table booked for one o'clock that is still going at five. The light coming down off the mountains in the last hours of the day. Travel measured in hours given back, not sights collected.
Our new journal piece on Franschhoek, Stellenbosch, and the case for the long lunch.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Lunch as the destination
item.description:
⟡ Lunch as the destination
Franschhoek takes its name from the French corner, after the Huguenots who arrived in 1688 with vine cuttings, surnames that still appear on the labels, and a way of eating that took its time. The valley kept the habit.
A good long lunch starts late and refuses to hurry. A table on a shaded stoep, courses arriving when they are ready rather than when the kitchen wants the table back, a bottle from the estate you are sitting on, and a view that does most of the talking. By the time coffee comes the afternoon has gone, and that was the plan.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Stay in the valley
item.description:
⟡ Stay in the valley
The common mistake is basing yourself in Cape Town and driving out for the day. An hour each way, a designated driver who cannot taste a thing, and a clock running over the whole table.
The Winelands reward the opposite. Stay in the valley. Give it days rather than hours. Let the afternoons run their natural length, with no drive home hanging over the last glass. Travelled this way, the region restores rather than depletes... and that is the version worth planning.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com
